body:
# Release Notes — v1.20.0 ## Enhancement ### Auto-remediation — rightsizing / config rule class across AWS, Azure & GCP XYZ auto-remediation expands beyond stop/idle into a new **rightsizing & config** rule class, taking the remediable-rule count to **~132**. Coverage spans **S3 intelligent-tiering / noncurrent-version / transfer-acceleration / deep-archive, ELB cross-zone, EBS snapshot archive, CloudWatch Logs retention, CloudFront cache TTL, Lambda timeout & concurrency, ASG floor, RDS backup-retention / PITR / gp3, Azure storage replication / tier / Log Analytics retention / Recovery Vault / MySQL config, GCP Cloud Run / Cloud SQL / GCS**, plus a generalized lifecycle template and an EC2 two-tier resize. All DB-config rules are **GUIDED** (type-to-confirm) only. Also unblocks **cold-resize** end-to-end: the executor honours the internal `remediation@XYZ` actor for the stop/start step, and the EC2 resize race that left actions stuck on `stopping` is fixed by waiting for the `stopped` state. ### Architecture & Cost Reports — unified tag picker (values grouped by key) The TagFilter on Architecture (Globe + Canvas) and Cost Reports → Tags now renders values **nested under collapsible key groups** with tri-state select-all, search-aware auto-expand, and a hover tooltip on truncated values — replacing the flat `env=prod / team=risk` list that was hard to scan at scale. Cost Reports → Tags also collapses its two cascading **All Keys + Pick-a-key-first** dropdowns into the same single picker, with cost shown per value as USD. --- ## Technical details
Aggregator ClickHouse dual-write & flag-gated reads (#1655) - **Dual-write seam lives inside the Store** — Handler → Service → Store is unchanged; writes go to MySQL **and** ClickHouse on every ingest; reads pick the engine at request time via `CLICKHOUSE_READS_ENABLED`. - **ClickHouse schema** uses `ReplacingMergeTree` so re-ingest is idempotent (same partition key collapses duplicates on merge), matching the MySQL upsert semantics. - **New migration** `20260608100000_create_clickhouse_cost_metrics` adds the CH-side tables; gated on `CH_HOST` being set so non-ClickHouse environments are unaffected. - **Byte-identical reads** verified via `shadow_compare` (same Service call → both engines → diff). Pinned in `clickhouse_test.go` (+4.5k LOC of fixture-driven equivalence tests across cost, allocation, breakdown, tag, team, and unit-metric paths). - **CH-only config**: `CH_HOST / CH_PORT / CH_USER / CH_PASSWORD / CH_DATABASE` belong **only** to the aggregator — do not add them to any other service. `scripts/local-dev.sh infra` now also starts ClickHouse so the flag-on path is locally exercisable. - **Rollback** = unset the flag (or set to `false`) and restart; MySQL stays authoritative, so no data risk.
Remediation — rightsizing/config rule class + cold-resize unblock (#1608) - **New `configure/*` adapters** wired into the `configure` registry: S3 (intelligent-tiering, noncurrent-version, transfer-acceleration, deep-archive), ELB (cross-zone), EBS (snapshot archive), CloudWatch Logs (retention), CloudFront (cache TTL), Lambda (timeout, concurrency), ASG (floor), RDS (backup-retention, PITR, gp3); Azure (storage replication, tier, Log-Analytics retention, recovery vault, MySQL); GCP (Cloud Run, Cloud SQL, GCS). - **`set_lifecycle_policy`** generalized to rule-id-keyed routing with per-rule copy. **Glue DPU** + **EC2 two-tier resize** added. - **All DB-config rules are GUIDED** (type-to-confirm) only — per the §12 narrow exemption. - **Build-time IAM guard (`iamguard`)** AST-checks the AWS adapter's SDK calls against the permissions catalog; Azure/GCP declare `RequiredPermissions()` with a read/write direction check — a missing or mis-prefixed IAM action now fails the build, not at apply time. - **Cold-resize unblock**: executor RBAC exemption for the internal `remediation@XYZ` actor on the stop/start step, and the EC2 resize race that left actions on `stopping` now waits for `stopped` before resizing. - **Permissions catalog** embeds regenerated for AWS / Azure / GCP (`docs/permissions/XYZ/*.json`). Frontend `GuidedReviewStep` tests cover the reversible-class banner.
Internal-API health-recorder cron + per-replica lock (#1681) - **Replaced** the goroutine + HTTP self-call (`StartHealthRecorder`) with a **Flux cron** (`health-recorder`, default `*/1 * * * *`, configurable via `HEALTH_RECORDER_CRON_SCHEDULE`) that calls `RecordHealthSnapshot(ctx)` directly in-process. README + ARCHITECTURE decision #34 updated; stale `ADMIN_API_KEY` / `HTTP_PORT` notes removed. - **Per-replica lock**: Flux crons fire on every replica, so the cron uses `RecordHealthSnapshotOnce` — a per-minute Redis `SetNX` lock (`XYZ:health:recorder:lock:`, 90s TTL) ensures only one replica writes per tick. Lock loss returns nil (another replica owns the minute); a Redis error surfaces so the next tick retries. - **Bounded runs**: each tick is wrapped in a 30s `context.WithTimeout` (derived from `ctx.Context`, not `*Flux.Context` itself — see comment in `main.go` on the recursion footgun), mirroring the previous HTTP timeout. - The `GET /api/components/health` endpoint and its handler are unchanged; the frontend still uses them. The handler's best-effort snapshot stays lock-free.
Console drawer-close URL-state fix (#1693) The drawer is URL-backed via `?rec=`. Closing previously cleared only the local `drawerRec` state, leaving `?rec=` in the URL — so the effect that opens the rec named by `?rec=` fired on the next render (`drawerRec` null, `drawerId` still set) and re-opened it. A shared `closeDrawer()` now clears both setters together; React batches the updates, so on the next render the open effect's `!drawerId` guard holds. Header X / Esc / backdrop (`onClose`) and Apply / Dismiss handlers all route through it. One-file change; no behavior change to opening or deep-linking.
Unified tag picker (#1699) - **TagFilter** gains nested key→value groups with collapsible headers, a tri-state group select-all, search-aware auto-expand, and a hover tooltip on truncated values. Two optional props added (backwards-compatible): `placeholder` (default `'Tags'`) and `formatMetric` (default `(item) => item.count`). - **TagShowback (`/cost-reports` → Tags tab)** replaces the cascading **All Keys / Pick-a-key-first** pair with the single grouped TagFilter; cost shown per value (USD via `formatMetric`). - Internal state migrated from two CSV strings (`keyFilter`, `valueFilter`) to one `selectedTags: {key, value}[]` array; the cascade-prune logic is deleted (orphans aren't possible with tuples). - E2E spec rewritten for the single-picker flow (`e2e/playground-showback-tags-multiselect.spec.js`).
Scope & rollback - `main...development` ships **#1608, #1655, #1681, #1693, #1699** on top of v1.19.2. - New DB migrations: - **CH-side (aggregator):** `20260608100000_create_clickhouse_cost_metrics` — gated on `CH_HOST`; non-CH environments unaffected. - **MySQL (recommender):** `20260609120000_add_resource_json_to_recommendations` — additive `resource_json JSON NULL` column on both `recommendations` and `recommendations_v2_validation`; NULL-able, no backfill required, safe to run against a live table. - New env vars: `CH_HOST / CH_PORT / CH_USER / CH_PASSWORD / CH_DATABASE / CLICKHOUSE_READS_ENABLED` (aggregator only), `HEALTH_RECORDER_CRON_SCHEDULE / TOPOLOGY_CRON_SCHEDULE / DASHBOARD_WARMUP_CRON_SCHEDULE` (internal-api, all default to existing cadence). `ADMIN_API_KEY` is no longer required for background health snapshots. - **#1685 (per-override display timezone)** was merged and then **reverted by #1696** within the same window — both commits land in `development`, net effect is zero on this release. - Rolling back ClickHouse = set `CLICKHOUSE_READS_ENABLED=false` and restart; MySQL stays authoritative.
